data.title: Wall Street ended mostly lower as investors eye Trump-Putin summit in Alaska
data.description:
Wall Street’s major averages ended mostly lower on Friday, as market participants have been keeping an eye on the highly anticipated meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) closed slightly higher by 0.07%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) ended lower by 0.29%, and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite concluded in the red as well as it handed back 0.40%.

“Whilst overlevered late longs may be found consoling themselves with a spirit-laced Celsius this evening, the prudent investor will be celebrating another green week; indeed a week where the Dow finally cheered up and joined the party,” Seeking Alpha’s analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research stated. 

“The boomer wins of UNH and INTC ran hot to close the week, dragging Grandpa Dow onto the dancefloor; rather bizarrely due to (i) the prospect of high-fiving retail investors bidding up UNH to get Berkshire Hathaway somewhere back nearer cost and (ii) a potential federal bailout of Intel.  At least it makes a change from AI,” King added.

From a sector vantage point, only four of the S&P 500’s 11 segments ended trading in positive territory, with Health Care and Real Estate at the top of the leaderboard. In reverse, Financials were the weakest performer on the day. 

Over in the bond market, U.S. Treasury yields moved up as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) pushed up 3 basis point to 4.32%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) inched up 2 basis points to 3.75%. 

Attention is now focused on Alaska, where Trump and Putin have started talks on what's expected to focus around the Russia Ukraine war. En route to the summit, Trump stated on the possibility of territorial swaps: 

"They'll be discussed, but I've got to let Ukraine make that decision... I'm not here to negotiate for Ukraine. I'm here to get them at a table." 

On the economic calendar, July retail sales rose slightly less than expected and core sales came in line with forecasts. Import prices rose more than expected in July, and the export prices moved up. 

Industrial production edged down in July while manufacturing output remained unchanged.

Additionally, the U.S. consumer sentiment index slid to 58.6 in August from 61.7 in July, and business inventories rose as expected, in the month of June.

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of UnitedHealth (UNH) popped 12% after Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway disclosed in a regulatory filing that it purchased more than 5M shares of the insurer last quarter. 

Applied Materials (AMAT), meanwhile, tumbled 13.9% on the day after delivering fourth-quarter guidance that came in well below Wall Street’s expectations.

data.title: Standard Chartered under pressure as Stefanik asks for DoJ probe
data.description:
Standard Chartered (OTCPK:SCBFF) is down ~6% after Rep. Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) requested that the Department of Justice launch an investigation into the bank over allegations of dodging sanctions placed on it.

Those restrictions are in regards to allegedly violating Iranian sanctions and not following anti-money laundering procedures. The UK-based bank was fined $1.1B in 2019 from regulators in the US and UK.

Stefanik's urgency is due to a deferred-prosecution agreement set to end on Aug. 19.

Stefanik's letter to Attorney General Pam Bondi states that "Standard Chartered Bank made at least $9.6 billion in illicit payments to known terrorists. China has been using Standard Chartered Bank to purchase sanctioned Iranian oil.

"These payments were hidden from required disclosure under the current deferred prosecution agreement being supervised by the Southern District of New York and the U.S. Attorney for Washington D.C."

Stefanik also criticized New York Attorney General Letitia James for not taking action against Standard Chartered in 2024 despite the allegations.

She also requested that acting U.S. Attorney for New Jersey, Alina Habba, lead the probe.

Standard Chartered (OTCPK:SCBFY) told MarketWatch that the allegations are "entirely false."

data.title: U.S. drilling rig count stays flat after string of declines in Baker Hughes survey
data.description:
The number of total oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. remained unchanged at 539, after the count had declined in 14 of the previous 15 weeks, Baker Hughes reported Friday in its latest weekly survey.

Oil rigs added 1 to 412 during the week, while gas rigs fell by 1 to 122 and 5 rigs continued to be classified as miscellaneous.

The total rig count was down 47 rigs, or 8% below this time last year.

In Texas, the biggest oil and gas producing state, the total rig count fell by 1 to 242, and in the Permian Basin, the top U.S. oil-producing shale formation, the total rig count lost 1 to 255, the lowest since September 2021 for both areas.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Bausch Health rises after $300M worth insider purchase (update)
data.description:
Bausch Health (NYSE:BHC) shares added ~4% on Friday after John Paulson, a board member of the Canadian pharma giant, disclosed the purchase of more than $300M worth of company shares. Bausch + Lomb (BLCO), a Bausch Health (NYSE:BHC) subsidiary in which Paulson holds another board position, also traded higher.

According to a regulatory filing on Thursday, Paulson, the President and Portfolio Manager of Paulson Capital, bought more than 34.7M shares of Bausch Health (NYSE:BHC) stock for an average of $9.00 apiece in a transaction worth roughly $312.5M.

The transaction conducted on Thursday has lifted Paulson’s stake in the eye care company by 96%. A day earlier, he had purchased about 3.2M BHC shares for an average price of approximately $6.56 apiece in a deal valued at $21.3M.

Meanwhile, according to an amended 13-D filing, billionaire investor Carl Icahn has liquidated his entire stake in Bausch Health (NYSE:BHC) with a sale of nearly 34.7M BHC shares on Thursday.

In a press release late Friday, Bausch Health (BHC) confirmed the transactions, noting that Paulson Capital Inc. and certain affiliates and funds managed by the firm acquired a net long position of about 34.7M shares held by Carl Icahn and his affiliates.

Paulson Capital and its affiliates now hold roughly 19% of the company’s common shares, and in connection with the deal, Brett Icahn and Steven Miller have resigned from Bausch Health’s (BHC) board.

Update 3.37 PM ET: Adds Bausch Health's update

data.title: Chemtrade climbs to five-year high after 'home run' $150M deal for Polytec
data.description:
Chemtrade Logistics Income Fund (OTC:CGIFF) +9.8% in Friday's trading to its highest since January 2020 after unveiling an agreement to acquire Polytec, a U.S.-based provider of turnkey water treatment solutions, for US$150M, in a deal Scotiabank called a "home run" that will drive instant growth.

Chemtrade (OTC:CGIFF) said the addition of Polytec will expand its footprint in water solutions for the food-processing industry and municipal markets while also adding to its range of products.

Scotiabank analyst Ben Isaacson upgraded Chemtrade (OTC:CGIFF) to Sector Outperform from Sector Perform with a C$15 price target, raised from C$13.50, calling the Polytec deal a "home run" and a way to diversify the company's customer base and expand market reach.

"We generally like the move out of ag/ferts and into slightly more cyclical chemical exposure," Isaacson wrote, seeing the deal adding 6%-7% instant growth to EBITDA without straining the balance sheet.

Chemtrade (OTC:CGIFF) also reported Logistics reported mixed Q2 results, with earnings that lagged expectations and sales that topped estimates.

data.title: Wave Life Sciences gains as JPMorgan suspends rating (update)
data.description:
Update 3:25pm: Adds company comment.

  • Wave Life Sciences (NASDAQ:WVE) rose 7.5% after JPMorgan suspended its rating, price target, and estimates on the company. 
  • Some investors may be speculating that JPMorgan suspended its rating because it's advising the company on some type of transaction. 
  • Eric Joseph, who covered Wave Life Sciences (NASDAQ:WVE) for JPMorgan, left the bank recently, Wave Life Sciences told Seeking Alpha in an email.

data.title: Savara upgraded at H.C. Wainwright on potential refiling of marketing application
data.description:
H.C. Wainwright upgraded Savara (NASDAQ:SVRA) to Buy from Neutral and raised its price target to $5 from $2 per share on Friday, citing a potential resubmission of the company’s marketing application for its lead asset, Molbreevi, with the FDA.

The agency issued a refusal to file in response to the Langhorne, Pennsylvania-based biotech’s biologics license application for Molbreevi in May, citing insufficient data related to its Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls.

However, H.C. Wainwright analyst Andrew Fein cites higher prospects for its revised BLA, expected to be refiled in December, with Fujifilm Diosynth Biotechnologies (OTCPK:FUJIY) (OTCPK:FUJIF) as its drug substance manufacturer rather than privately held GEMA biotech.

He also noted a Type A meeting in which Savara (NASDAQ:SVRA) has reached alignment with the FDA regarding the CMC data needed for the BLA’s resubmission, which the company targets for a priority review with a target action date potentially in August 2026.

Fein added that “considerable data generated from the Process Performance Qualification (PPQ) campaigns at Fuji and alignment with the FDA regarding both the analytical comparability analysis and the CMC data that will be submitted” with the BLA lessens the risk of another refusal.

data.title: Citi credit card delinquency rate drifts up, net charge-offs slide in July
data.description:
Citigroup's (NYSE:C) credit card delinquencies advanced sequentially in July, while net-charge offs retreated over the same period, the bank disclosed on Friday. Still, both metrics trailed their pre-pandemic marks.

The Citibank Credit Card Master Trust delinquency rate of 1.42% rose from 1.38% in June and slid from 1.44% in the year-ago period. It's also down from the prepandemic level of 1.53% in July 2019.

The net charge-off rate was 2.07% in July, down from 2.12% in the prior month and 2.59% a year earlier, according to a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission filing. The rate was 2.91% six 2019.

Lending activity slowed further last month, with principal receivables falling to $20.7B at the end of the July period from $20.9B a month before.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Eyes on Jackson Hole, Walmart report, Google event, and Foot Locker-Dick's vote
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - August 18

  • Volatility watch - Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) and Tilray (TLRY) set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Celcuity (CELC), Rigel Pharma (RIGL), and Indivior (INDV). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Spirit Aviation (FLYY), Vtex (VTEX), and Monday.com (MNDY).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Palo Alto Networks (PANW) and Fabrinet (FN).
  • IPO watch - The IPO quiet period expires on NIQ Global Intelligence (NIQ) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Wynn Resorts (WYNN), Applied Materials (AMAT), and Microsoft (MSFT).
  • All day - Lucid Group (LCID) will conduct a special meeting to hold a shareholder vote to approve a proposed 1-for-10 reverse stock split.
  • 11 a.m. - Sitio Royalties (STR) holders will hold vote on sale to Viper  Energy (VNOM).

 

Tuesday - August 19

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Home Depot (HD), XPeng (XPEV). Options trading implies a strong move for Viking Holdings (VIK) after its report is released.
  • All day - Companies due to participate at the closely watched EnerCom Denver Energy Investment Conference include EOG Resources (EOG), Sandridge Energy (SD), and Oklo (OKLO).
  • All day - The Rosenblatt Age of AI Technology Summit will take place. The gathering is recognized as a key event for tracking developments in the AI semiconductor, networking, and infrastructure ecosystem. Some of the companies participating include Nvidia (NVDA), Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI), Fabrinet (FN), and Rambus (RMBS).

 

Wednesday - August 20

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Target (TGT), Lowe's (LOW), Baidu (BIDU), TJX Companies (TJX), Estee Lauder (EL), and Analog Devices (ADI).
  • All day - Companies due to participate at the Needham Virtual Semiconductor & Semicap Conference include Ultra Clean Holdings (UCTT), Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS), and Tower Semiconductor (TSEM).
  • 1:00 p.m. Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) will hold a Pixel event from New York City with a live YouTube stream. The tech giant will unveil the Pixel 10 lineup, Pixel Watch 4, Pixel Buds 2a, and accessories featuring the new Tensor G5 chip. Android 16 QPR1 with Material 3 Expressive design will also debut. In addition, Google is expected to showcase AI-powered features, camera upgrades, and exclusive Google Store offers, with possible surprise product announcements beyond the confirmed devices.
  • 2:00 p.m. - The Federal Reserve will release the minutes of its last meeting.
  • 2:00 p.m. - Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic will discuss the economic outlook in a moderated conversation before the Fintech South 2025 event.

 

Thursday - August 21

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Walmart (NYSE:WMT), Intuit (INTU). Workday (WDAY), and Ross Stores (ROST).
  • All day - The Federal Reserve's Jackson Hole Symposium will be closely watched for indications of monetary policy changes. This year's theme is "Labor Markets in Transition: Demographics, Productivity, and Macroeconomic Policy." 
  • All day - Costco (COST) will open a new store in Las Vegas. The location will be the sixth Costco location in the Las Vegas Valley and the first new one since 2018.
  • All day - It is the FDA action date on Ionis Pharmaceuticals' (IONS) Donidalorsen for hereditary angioedema.
  • All day - Disney's (DIS) ESPN will launch its new direct-to-consumer streaming service offering access to all of ESPN's linear networks plus ESPN+, SEC Network+, and ACCNX. Priced at $29.99 per month, the service will feature 47,000 live events annually, on-demand replays, studio shows, and original programming.
  • All day - Global flash PMIs for August will be released. The resilience of the global economy amid new U.S. tariffs will be in focus after the July manufacturing PMI fell below 50 in the U.S.
  • 10:00 a.m. - Foot Locker (NYSE:FL) will hold an extraordinary shareholders meeting to vote on the proposed merger with Dick's Sporting Goods (NYSE:DKS).
  • 10:00 a.m. - Trial with Federal Trade Commission trying to block GTCR's purchase of Surmodics (SRDX) set to begin.

 

Friday - August 22

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include BJ's Wholesale (BJ) and RLX Technology (RLX).
  • All day - Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will give a highly anticipated speech in Jackson Hole focusing on the economic outlook and the Fed's policy framework. Powell is not expected to address the pressure from the White House on shaping interest rate policy.
  • All day - Bandwidth (BAND) will present at the Piper Sandler Voice AI Day event.

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this article.

data.title: PepsiCo under spotlight as activist target after 13F filings
data.description:
PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP) may be a possible target of activist investors amid some recent 13F filings. 

A 13F filing from Japan's Nomura on Thursday indicated that the brokerage had acquired more than 4 million shares during Q2. Nomura is a noteworthy because it's viewed as a counterparty for activist swap positions.

The Nomura position comes after Mizuho showed a new position in PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP) a couple weeks ago, Gordon Haskett's Don Bilson highlighted in a note on Friday. In addition, Barclays bought a "pretty big slug" in Q2, according to a 13F filing earlier this week, which showed a 3.3 share addition during the quarter.

It's not clear what an activist might be looking to do with PepsiCo (PEP), though they may want the company to split up or maybe the board may need an overhaul, Bilson speculated. 

Shares of PepsiCo (PEP) rose 1.4% on Friday. The stock has dropped 13% over the past year, while Coca-Cola has gained 2.3%.
